style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;overflow=hidden;rounded=0;" value="<h1 style="margin-top: 0px;">Technical Role</h1><p><b>Function:&nbsp;</b></p><p></p><ul><li><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">Scope, create, document, maintain, support &amp; decommission data &amp; analytical products, across multiple business areas</span></li><li><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">Provide technical expertise in the sizing of requested work</span></li></ul><p></p><p></p>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="135" width="530" x="20" y="930" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="4XZdbzBWn-lD2Hodhysp-80" parent="4XZdbzBWn-lD2Hodhysp-15" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;overflow=hidden;rounded=0;" value="<h1 style="margin-top: 0px;">Portfolio Manager / Product Owner</h1><p><b>Function:&nbsp;</b></p><p></p><ul><li><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">Customer / Account management between technical services and specific business area</span></li><ul><li>Co-ordination with upcoming projects &amp; initiatives</li><li>Stakeholder mapping / customer profiles</li><li>Access control management (technical resources)</li></ul><li>Lifecycle management of data &amp; analytical products for a specific business area</li><ul><li>Agile delivery planning &amp; task development</li><ul><li>Participate in Sprint Planning &amp; Resource Allocation bidding</li><ul><li>Resource points proportional to size &amp; complexity of portfolio</li></ul></ul><li>Release management &amp; change communications</li><li>Resource allocation</li></ul><li>Administrative &amp; Support</li><ul><li>HR functions, <i>i.e. employee management, leave, training, development, ...</i></li></ul></ul><p></p>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="260" width="530" x="20" y="650" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="4XZdbzBWn-lD2Hodhysp-82" parent="1" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;overflow=hidden;rounded=0;" value="<h1 style="margin-top: 0px;">Operating Model</h1><p><b>Business capabilities of data engineering and business intelligence are provided by Technical Services function.</b></p><p>Delivery of capabilities is coordinated by a group of business-area aligned Portfolio Managers / Product Owners, who are able to pull required technical resources from a business-area agnostic talent pool, depending on the phase, task type and stage of the required works.</p><p>This model allows;</p><p></p><ul><li>Portfolio&nbsp;Managers&nbsp;to maintain long-term relationships with their respective business areas, enabling deep integration with local processes, approval flows, technical SMEs, domain-specific context, technology stacks, and release format preferences</li><li>Technical resources to provide function specific skills to projects / initiatives / efforts where they are required, and to build knowledge &amp; familiarity with a wide range of business functions &amp; domains</li><li>Technical resources are allocated dynamically, limiting the slack capacity within the capability pool</li><li>Decoupling technical resources from business areas allows the function to&nbsp;</li><ul><li>scale locally and holistically as demand scales</li><li>promote strong &amp; consistent documentation across business areas</li><li>promote strong &amp; consistent processes&nbsp;across business areas</li><li>leverage consistent toolsets &amp; lessons learnt across business areas</li><li>ensure technical resource available, smoothing the impact of individual unavailability</li><li>promote non-user linked processes &amp; connectivity, reducing risk of SPOF &amp; knowledge loss</li><li>uplift overall technology &amp; process maturity</li><li>ensure collaboration &amp; mutual support between technical resources</li><li>provide opportunities for training, mentoring &amp; staff mobility within the consolidated function</li><li>promotes strong documentation, knowledge, and handover / asset management practices</li></ul><li>Encourages&nbsp;business areas to develop &amp; scope their requirements before engaging / in partnership with Technical Services</li><li>Encourages business areas to accept product development lifecycle components, i.e. regular (but controlled) releases, rather than ad-hoc &amp; continuous tweaks.&nbsp;</li></ul><div><b style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">Governance components:</b></div><div><ul><li>Business area maintain ownership of catalogs, products, documentation, systems, access management &amp; audit responsibilities</li><li>Technical services are given devolved authority to create, maintain &amp; decommission assets for the business areas, by relevant business area Information Asset Custodians / Stewards</li></ul><div>i.e.&nbsp;<span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">Business area Information Asset Custodians / Stewards grant Portfolio Managers devolved authority to manage technical resources' access to their assets, for the purpose of creating, maintaining and decommissioning Data &amp; Analytical products. Portfolio Managers are then responsible for rotating in the relevant technical resources to the relevant system &amp; documentation libraries roles groups based on what work is required.</span></div></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="657" width="610" x="1320" y="-1687" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="4XZdbzBWn-lD2Hodhysp-83" parent="1" li.span: Scope, create, document, maintain, support & decommission data & analytical products, across multiple business areas
Provide technical expertise in the sizing of requested work

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 528px; height: 1px; padding-top: 1547px; margin-left: 22px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; max-height: 256px; overflow: hidden; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

li.span: Customer / Account management between technical services and specific business area

ul.li: Co-ordination with upcoming projects & initiatives
Stakeholder mapping / customer profiles
Access control management (technical resources)
Lifecycle management of data & analytical products for a specific business area
Agile delivery planning & task development
Participate in Sprint Planning & Resource Allocation bidding
Resource points proportional to size & complexity of portfolio
Release management & change communications
Resource allocation
Administrative & Support
HR functions, i.e. employee management, leave, training, development, ...

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 608px; height: 1px; padding-top: 10px; margin-left: 1312px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; max-height: 653px; overflow: hidden; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

p.b: Business capabilities of data engineering and business intelligence are provided by Technical Services function.

div.p: Delivery of capabilities is coordinated by a group of business-area aligned Portfolio Managers / Product Owners, who are able to pull required technical resources from a business-area agnostic talent pool, depending on the phase, task type and stage of the required works.

ul.li: Portfolio Managers to maintain long-term relationships with their respective business areas, enabling deep integration with local processes, approval flows, technical SMEs, domain-specific context, technology stacks, and release format preferences

Technical resources to provide function specific skills to projects / initiatives / efforts where they are required, and to build knowledge & familiarity with a wide range of business functions & domains
Technical resources are allocated dynamically, limiting the slack capacity within the capability pool
Decoupling technical resources from business areas allows the function to
scale locally and holistically as demand scales
promote strong & consistent documentation across business areas
promote strong & consistent processes across business areas
leverage consistent toolsets & lessons learnt across business areas
ensure technical resource available, smoothing the impact of individual unavailability
promote non-user linked processes & connectivity, reducing risk of SPOF & knowledge loss
uplift overall technology & process maturity
ensure collaboration & mutual support between technical resources
provide opportunities for training, mentoring & staff mobility within the consolidated function
promotes strong documentation, knowledge, and handover / asset management practices
Encourages business areas to develop & scope their requirements before engaging / in partnership with Technical Services
Encourages business areas to accept product development lifecycle components, i.e. regular (but controlled) releases, rather than ad-hoc & continuous tweaks.
Business area maintain ownership of catalogs, products, documentation, systems, access management & audit responsibilities
Technical services are given devolved authority to create, maintain & decommission assets for the business areas, by relevant business area Information Asset Custodians / Stewards

div.div: i.e. Business area Information Asset Custodians / Stewards grant Portfolio Managers devolved authority to manage technical resources' access to their assets, for the purpose of creating, maintaining and decommissioning Data & Analytical products. Portfolio Managers are then responsible for rotating in the relevant technical resources to the relevant system & documentation libraries roles groups based on what work is required.
